instruction: Do not start Phase 2 yet. First fix the current Phase 1 Flutter foundation so it compiles and matches the actual FastAPI backend contract.

workspace.status: Flutter Phase 1 foundation exists but contains compile blockers and API-contract mismatches.

review_findings.issue: Router imports screens that do not exist, so the app cannot compile.
details: Currently the imported files do not exist in lib/.

review_findings.issue: Login screen uses Colors.slate, which is not a valid Flutter Material color constant.
details: Replace Colors.slate.shade600 with an explicit Color value or theme color.

review_findings.issue: Generator model does not match the actual GET /api/generators response shape.
details: Backend returns keys id and capacity, not generator_id and capacity_kva, for generator list items.
rental_vendor_id is a string in the backend response, not int.
The current model will fail or decode incorrect values when the generator list API is used.

review_findings.issue: Vendor and RentalVendor models do not match the actual vendor API response shape.
details: GET /api/vendors returns id, name, place, phone.
GET /api/rental-vendors returns rental_vendor_id, name, place, phone.
Vendor ids are strings in the backend, not ints.
The current code expects vendor_id, vendor_name, vendor_place for retailer vendors, which is wrong for the list API.

review_findings.issue: Auth bootstrap marks the app authenticated based only on token presence, even if no user payload exists or the token is stale.
details: If token exists but user is missing or invalid, _status becomes authenticated and _user may remain null.
401 handling clears storage but does not notify AuthProvider, so the UI can stay in an inconsistent authenticated state.
Router redirect logic should treat the initial auth restoration state separately instead of immediately redirecting based on a transient unauthenticated state.

requirements: Either create the missing screen files under the imported feature paths or remove those imports and route references until real files exist.

required_fixes.task: Fix invalid Flutter UI code in login screen.
requirements: Replace Colors.slate usage with a valid color source.
Keep the visual style aligned with the web app.

required_fixes.task: Align the Flutter DTOs/models to the real backend API contracts.
requirements: Create separate DTOs if needed for list responses versus detail responses.
Use string ids for vendor ids, rental vendor ids, and generator ids.
For generator list responses, decode id and capacity correctly.
For vendor list responses, decode id/name/place/phone correctly.
Do not invent fields the backend does not return in that endpoint.

required_fixes.task: Harden auth restoration and logout behavior.
requirements: Treat auth initialization as a real loading state.
Only transition to authenticated when both token and valid user context are present.
If auth restoration fails, clear persisted auth cleanly.
When a 401 occurs in Dio, propagate logout back into AuthProvider so the UI updates immediately.
Do not implement vendors/generators/bookings business features yet.
It is acceptable to add minimal placeholder screens if needed for routing and compile safety.
Foundation must be correct before Phase 2 starts.

acceptance_criteria: The Flutter app compiles without unresolved imports.
The router boots with real or temporary placeholder screens in the declared feature paths.
The login screen no longer contains invalid Flutter color API usage.
Generator, vendor, and rental vendor decoding matches the actual backend list endpoints.
Auth initialization has a stable loading state and does not falsely mark the user authenticated from token-only state.
A 401 from the API can drive the app back to unauthenticated state cleanly.

after_completion: Summarize exactly which finding each change fixes.
State whether Phase 1 is now complete and safe to continue into Phase 2.
